channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Former Philippine President Duterte arrested on an ICC warrant over drug killings
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was arrested at Manila's international airport Tuesday on order of the International Criminal Court in connection with a case of crime against humanity filed against him, the Philippine government said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Arrested Philippine ex-President Duterte to face legacy of thousands killed in drug crackdown
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine ex-President Rodrigo Duterte, who left a savage legacy over his deadly anti-drugs crackdown, was arrested on a warrant issued by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity for the thousands killed in a war he waged against drugs in a political
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian air defenses shot down 337 Ukrainian drones over 10 regions, Russia's Defense Ministry says
item.description: The Russian military says air defenses overnight shot down 337 Ukrainian drones over 10 Russian regions in what appears to be the biggest Ukrainian drone attack on Russia in three years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: For Trump the peace negotiator, might makes right. History offers different lessons
item.description: As President Donald Trump seeks to end wars in the Middle East and Ukraine, his approach to both seems to boil down to giving the stronger party what it wants and pushing the weaker to accept it.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario premier says he needs 'to sit down as quickly as possible' with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday that he needs "to sit down as quickly as possible" with President Trump regarding tariffs. ‘We're seeing inflation happening already, and it's unnecessary. We don't need to do this. We can have the two most prosperous countries in the world.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rubio suggests Ukraine won't return to pre-2014 borders
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters Monday that Ukraine was unlikely to win back all of the territory captured by Russia since 2014, as U.S. and Ukrainian officials are set to meet for talks in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Canada's next prime minister Carney says transition will be quick after meeting with Trudeau
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s next prime minister met with outgoing prime minister Justin Trudeau on Monday and vowed a quick transition. Mark Carney said he had a long meeting with Trudeau in which they discussed U.S.-Canada relations, national security issues and the timing of the handover in power.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says mineral deal ‘not main topic on agenda’ in Ukraine meeting
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio and a U.S. delegation will meet with Ukrainian leaders on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss ways to end the war between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mark Kelly fires back at Musk over 'traitor' accusation
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) hit back at tech billionaire Elon Musk after he called Kelly a "traitor" for visiting Ukraine and demanding the United States stand with Ukrainians in their ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: GOP's Bacon: Trump repeating Taliban mistake in Ukraine talks
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said President Trump is repeating mistakes he made in Afghanistan by not including Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in talks to end his country’s war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: US-flagged tanker collides with container ship near UK
item.description: Thirty-two people were brought ashore, conditions unknown, after a vessel collided with a tanker carrying Jet-A1 fuel causing multiple explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea fires several ballistic missiles after the US and South Korea began military drills
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea fired several ballistic missiles into the sea Monday, South Korea’s military said, hours after South Korean and U.S. troops kicked off their large annual combined drills, which the North views as an invasion rehearsal.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is signaling that the Trump administration has finished its six-week purge of programs of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syria's government signs a breakthrough deal with Kurdish-led authorities in the northeast
item.description: Syria’s interim government has reached a deal with the Kurdish-led authority that controls the country’s northeast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A cache of explosives in Sydney provoked antisemitic terrorism fears. Police now say it was faked
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — An explosives-filled trailer that Australian politicians earlier described as an antisemitic terrorism plot and a foiled mass casualty event was staged by criminals in a complicated hoax -- and was never meant to be detonated, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Philippine President Duterte shrugs off possible arrest by ICC for drug war during trip to Hong Kong
item.description: Ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte visited Hong Kong on Sunday, leading to speculation that he may be attempting to evade a possible arrest warrant by the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mark Carney wins Liberal Party nomination to replace Trudeau as Canada's next PM
item.description: Former Goldman Sachs executive Mark Carney won the Liberal Party primary on Sunday and will be appointed Canada's next prime minister after the resignation of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump critic Mark Carney poised to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney’s challenge will be to differentiate himself from Trudeau and take on Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre. If Carney wins he's expected to call for an early general election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis now eating solid foods, showing 'slight gradual improvements,' Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis is responding well to treatment and has shown a "gradual, mild improvement" in recent days as he continues his recovery from double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' during fourth week in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis has apparently shown "slight improvement" and is responding will to oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday. This is the pope's fourth week at Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Scottish golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestine group over Gaza stance
item.description: President Trump’s Turnberry golf resort in Scotland was vandalized with graffiti by pro-Palestine activists who are protesting his plans for Gaza and to displace Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea unveils its first nuclear-powered submarine
item.description: North Korea revealed its first nuclear-powered submarine, posing a threat to the United States and South Korea. Kim Jong Un has inspected its construction progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza plan criticized by US and Israel gets European support
item.description: Foreign ministers from France, Germany, Italy and the UK announced their support for Egypt's Gaza reconstruction plan, despite Israel and the US expressing reservations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN draft report on children in conflict zones raises eyebrows with framing of Israelis
item.description: The U.N.'s annual report on children in conflict zones, which is set to be published in June, is already getting attention for its framing of Israeli victims and for omitting Hamas crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, WGN 9

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis resting during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: The pope has been sleeping with a non-invasive mechanical mask to guarantee that his lungs expand properly overnight and help his recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Israel’s cutoff of supplies to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid stockpiles dwindle
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
